reasoning: Bupirimate is a pyrimidine fungicide containing a sulfonamide/sulfonylurea-like moiety with the structure 5-butyl-2-(ethylamino)-6-methylpyrimidin-4-yl dimethylsulfamate; however, it contains a sulfonate/sulfamate group on a pyrimidine ring. More precisely, bupirimate is a dimethylsulfamate ester of a hydroxypyrimidine, classifiable under sulfonyls/sulfonylurea-related organosulfur compounds. The defining feature is the sulfonamide (dimethylaminosulfonyl) group attached to a pyrimidine, best fitting Sulfonylureas at the direct parent level.
